system.effect: Time: Rebirth requires 2d6 turns to cast. It cannot be channeled. In this vile act of necromancy, the Sorcerer steals a dead person's soul, plucking it out of the halls of the dead. This is one of the darkest dark magic spells in existence, because the gods of the dead see it as an insult. The spell does not require a dead body: the magic will materialize the dead soul in new flesh. However, this spell is highly risky. Upon a successful casting, throw 2d6 on the Rebirth Table: (2) The gods refuse to let go of the soul and whoever the sorcerer is attempting to bring back must remain dead for eternity instead. No further attempts may be made to bring the person back to life. (3-4) The person indeed arises from the dead, but instead of rising as a normal mortal, the eternal powers of Chaos curse them with undeath! The person rises as a ghoul, under the Referee's control, who will attack any nearby characters and attempt to eat them. (5-6) The spell fails. Further rebirth attempts may be made at no penalty to the Sorcery roll, but all rolls on this table are made at a -2 penalty. Penalties are cumulative for repeated attempts: for example, a third attempt after failing twice will suffer from a -6 penalty. (7-9) The character is restored to life, but in a bad state. They will require 1d6 months of rest. Roll 1d6: 1-2, the character loses their memory; 3-4, the character is part alive and part dead; 5-6, the character will suffer from sudden and unexpected fits of murderous rage. (10) The character is restored to life, though still bearing terrible injuries. They will require 1d6 months of rest. Roll 1d6: 1-2 the character suffers from partial memory loss; 3-4, the character has passed through Hell and will have severe recurring nightmares; 5-6, the character undergoes a profound personality change as they recover. (11) The character is restored to life and will make a full recovery within 2 weeks. Roll 1d6: 1-2, the character will be sickly for the rest of their life; 3-4, the character suffers characteristic loss; 5-6, the character remains cold as a corpse and will suffer sudden shivers. (12+) The character is blessed by Chaos and awakens fully restored with full characteristics, ready to adventure at a moment's notice! Rebirth Effects — Characteristic Loss: the character loses 1d6 points from one characteristic, chosen randomly, to a minimum of 1. Memory Loss, Full: The character suffers from complete amnesia, though they will retain basic life skills and partial adventuring capabilities. The character loses half of their skill levels, chosen by the Referee. The character's EDU characteristic is halved (round up). Memory Loss, Partial: The character loses part of their memory but retains a general idea of their identity, as well as some adventuring capabilities. The character loses one skill entirely, regardless of level, chosen randomly. The character also loses 1d6 points of EDU. Partially Alive: The character is neither alive nor dead, and teeters constantly on death's door. They are kept alive only by black magic but at the same time are constantly called back to the afterlife (or hell) by the gods of death. The character suffers a -2 penalty to all actions and only has half their movement rate. The character must also succeed at an END 8+ throw every month, with the above penalty, lest the character dies and the gods of the underworld reclaim their lost soul. Personality Change: The character retains their memories, but his or her personality will change in a profound manner, as agreed upon by player and Referee. Recurring Nightmares: The character suffers from memories of being dead in a dreadful afterlife – as well as other negative visions. They will invariable suffer from severe recurring nightmares every night, preventing healthy sleep. The character will suffer severe insomnia as a result, causing a perpetual -1 modifier to all actions. Sickly: The character's body does not fully recover from being dead and its life force is far weaker. Natural healing takes twice as long, and so does magical healing based on natural healing times (such as Respite spells). The character also suffers a -1 penalty to throws to resist disease, poison, or acid. Sudden Murderous Rage: The character awakens as normal but is shadowed by a demon. At the Referee's discretion, usually once a week or month, the character must make throw INT 8+ or fly into murderous bloodlust lasting 2d6 rounds, doing their best to kill any living being within reach. After this period, they will return to normal, but will not remember any of their actions while enraged. A sorcerer may attempt to use Exorcism to banish the demon. In this case, the character throws INT 8+. If successful, the sorcerer drives away the demon and prevents any further bouts of rage; if failed, the character becomes permanently controlled by the demon – and from that point on is considered an NPC monster controlled by the Referee.
system.critSuccess: Add the Sorcery throw's Effect is added to the Rebirth Table.
